title: This YouTube Keyword Research Trick Got 775K Views

transcript.text: Good news, the YouTube algorithm does still love small channels. A channel with literally 93 subscribers just pulled off 3/4 of a million views on a video about a 5-year-old indie rally video game. And the reason is so simple, it's a bit annoying actually, so stick with me. He didn't chase the keyword a platinum trophy. That search term gets over 150,000 searches a month, but you're competing against every massive YouTube channel on Earth for it. Instead, he went after the term Art of Rally. 50,000 searches a month, but a competition score of just 30 out of 100. That's real people searching, but basically nobody serving them. And that's not luck, that's an opportunity you can find with VidIQ in under 30 seconds. Now, obviously the video had to be good as well, and the comments confirm that. A few days of positive signals and whoosh, the video completely blew up. So, find the game that everybody loves, but nobody is covering, and make the video a quest, not a checklist, which will let the algorithm be as kind to you as it was to him.
